Off Calendar Sep-12-2011 Continued To Oct-03-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ed Koplowitz for amd with Michael Levy.

Mr. Koplowitz requested a three week continuance stating that they reached an agreement, that the written agreement will go out to Mr. Spiegel today, that they will not file a petition for approval of the settlement and that dismissals will be filed. The Petition for Further Relief Regarding Disgorgment for Attorney's Fees and Damages was continued to October 3, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Off Calendar Jun-27-2011 Continued To Aug-29-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Spiegel for Claude Levy. Michael Levy in Propria Persona.

The original bank statements were returned to Mr. Levy in open Court. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation (order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation signed in open Court). Mediation is to be completed by August 26, 2011. The matter was continued to August 29, 2011 at 9:00 a.m, in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Petition For Further Relief Regarding Disgorgment For Attorneys Fees And For Damages
Off Calendar Jun-13-2011 Continued To Jun-27-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Spiegel for Claude Levy (Petitioner). Michael Levy in Propria Persona.

Mr. Spiegel stated that the accounting documents appear to be adequate documents but that he has not had the opportunity to have the Accountant review it. Mr. Spiegel also questioned a $80,000.00 increase in the corpus of the Trust in 2005. The accounting was taken under submission. Mr. Spiegel stated that he intends to conduct discovery if the damages matter goes to trial. Mr. Levy stated that he was not sure what discovery he would conduct. The petition regarding disgorgment/fees/damages was continued to June 27, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow the Parties time to decide if they are amenable to mediation.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy K. Sandberg.

Petition For Further Relief Regarding Disgorgment For Attorneys Fees And For Damages
Off Calendar May-23-2011 Continued To Jun-13-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Spiegel for Claude Levy. Michael Levy in Propria Persona.

Mr. Levy presented a copy of the Credit Suisse bank statement from April 30, 2005. The Court informed Mr. Levy that the statemnet as of April 15, 2005 was needed. Mr. Levy is to file and serve the Bank statement for the period ending March 30, 2005, contact Credit Suisse Bank and request the amount on hand on April 15, 2005 through the end of September 2005 as well as the amount on hand on October 13, 2005 (a Bank letter is acceptable). The Court also instructed Mr. Spiegel to use Mr. Levy's mailing address for service. The matters were continued to June 13, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

Petition For Further Relief Regarding Disgorgment For Attorneys Fees And For Damages
Off Calendar Apr-25-2011 Continued To May-23-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Spiegel for Claude Levy (Petitioner). Michael Levy in Propria Persona.

The Petition for Further Relief Regarding Disgorgment for Attorney's Fees and for Damages, and the Accounting Petition were continued to May 23, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court wants to see all originial bank statements from the Swiss Bank account from April 15, 2005 to present/time of split, the original statements for May of 2001 and for June 2001. The Court directed Mr. Levy to lodge all of the original bank statements with the Probate Examiner, to serve copies of everything on Mr. Speigel and to file any documents by noon Friday May 6, 2011. Mr. Speigel is to file a written response by the end of the day May 18, 2011.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Apr-25-2011 Continued To May-23-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Further Relief Regarding Disgorgment For Attorneys Fees And For Damages
Off Calendar Mar-14-2011 Continued To Apr-25-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Spiegel for Claude Levy. Michael Levy in propria persona.

The Court stated before it today were two petitions: the first and final account by Michael Levy and the petition for disgorgement. The Court noted that the only issue left in the petition for disgorgement is the fees. Mr. Spiegel stated that there was also the breach of fiduciary duties. Both parties stated that they did not receive the notes. The Court stated that Michael Levy finally transferred the assets to Claude. The Court then addressed the issue with the account stating that it doesn't deal with the window of time that Michael Levy began to act under the power of attorney (4/15/05 to 10/13/05), the payment to SJC Tax consulting, and the costs and expenses Michael Levy paid himself for defending this matter. Mr. Levy stated that he believed that the accounting was split; he'd look into it and that the SJC Tax consultation was regarding accounts that weren't reported previously. Mr. Spiegel stated that there was the issue of the missing six months with regard to the accounting, that there were no certified copies of bank statements which their forensic accountant (Mr. Sugarman) had requested, that Michael Levy mismanaged the Estate and produced no income according to Mr. Sugarman, that Michael Levy failed to pay Claude's medical insurance in 2007 for whatever reason, and that all of this is a breach of fiduciary duties. Mr. Levy stated that he did not know the date of the split, that Claude would not go to the doctor so he stopped his medical insurance which cost $600.00 per month, and that he hasn't seen Claude since 2007. Mr. Spiegel stated that they recovered $260,000.00, that they don't know if this amount is correct, that Credit Suisse won't talk to anyone that isn't the prinicipal, that they believe Michael Levy co-mingled funds, that they have no way to investigate if Claude Levy could have been the principal, and that Michael Levy has stonewalled this matter. The Court continued the matter to 4/25/11, ordered Michael Levy to file a supplement which is to cover the account period of 4/15/05 to 10/13/05, is to address in writing the SJC Tax consulting, and is to explain payment to himself for defending this matter. The Court ordered the parties to meet and confer about the Credit Suisse account by the end of this week. Michael Levy's supplmental accounting is due by the end of business on 4/19/11 and any response is due 4/21/11 by noon. The Court continued both matters to 4/25/11 and stated that it hoped to take both matters under submission on that date.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Mar-14-2011 Continued To Apr-25-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Further Relief Regarding Disgorgment For Attorneys Fees And For Damages
Off Calendar Feb-01-2011 Continued To Mar-14-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Spiegel for Claude Levy. Michael Levy in Propria Persona.

Mr. Speigel stated that the funds have arrived into the Trust account, that they still have the issue of breach of fiduciary duties and the objections to the accounting, and that there was no response to the objetions. Mr. Levy stated that he intends to file a response. Mr. Levy's response is to be filed by February 28, 2010. The matter was continued to March 14, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the remaining issues. The request for contempt is discharged without prejudice to Mr. Spiegel's request for attorney's fees on the pending petition before Commissioner Kaplan.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Further Relief Regarding Disgorgment For Attorneys Fees And For Damages
Off Calendar Jan-11-2011 Continued To Feb-01-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Spiegel for Claude Levy. Michael Levy in Propria Persona.

Mr. Levy stated that he had a document showing that the financial institution had received instructions to transfer the funds to Mr. Speigel's trust account, that he advised them that he was no longer in charge of the account and that the transfer would take two weeks to complete. The Court expressed concern about the situation and stated that there was nothing before it to indicate that Claude Levy lacked capacity. The matter was continued to February 1, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Counsel is to work out the communication issue.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Order To Show Cause: Contempt
Off Calendar Jan-11-2011 Continued To Feb-01-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Jan-11-2011 Continued To Feb-01-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Further Relief Regarding Disgorgment For Attorneys Fees And For Damages
Off Calendar Jan-05-2011 Continued To Jan-11-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Spiegel for Claude Levy. Michael Levy in Proria Persona.

Mr. Levy stated that only $9,000.00 had been distributed to Claude Levy, expressed concern about Claude's finances, his rent not being current, a new Power of Attorney being signed while Claude was hospitalized, Steven Sodokoff being related to Mr. Spiegel and suggested the Court review Claude's medical records to determine if Claude had capacity. The Court stated that the order issued by Commissioner Kaplan stands and that the funds are to be turned over by Friday. Mr. Levy stated that he would submit the request to the Swiss financial institution, that there may be some losses because the funds were investments, that he was not sure if the financial institution would cooperate with him as he no longer has Power of Attorney and that he had grave concerns about Claude's well being. Mr. Speigel stated that Michael Levy had not complied with the Court's order or with the probate code, and that he had not met his obligation as a fiduciary. The matter was continued to January 11, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Spiegel for Claude Levy. Michael Levy in Propria Persona.

Mr. Levy stated that the only account that had not been turned over was the Swiss account and that he needed bank account information from Mr. Spiegel so that the funds could be transferred. Commissioner Kaplan informed Mr. Spiegel that she does not have contempt powers. Mr. Spiegle stated that he was still pursuing the contempt matter. Petition For Disgorgement For Removal Of Attorney In Fact And For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Oct-04-2010 Text Ruling

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Account By Michael Levy
Off Calendar Oct-04-2010 Continued To Nov-29-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Spiegel for Claude Levy. Michael Levy in propria persona.

The Court stated that there were three petitions before it today: 1) a petition to compel accounting which was heard on 8/9/10, that it ordered an accounting on that date, that it never received the order from Mr. Spiegel, and that Mr. Spiegel still needs to submit the order for signature 2) a petition for disgorgement, for removal, and for fees, and 3) a petition for further relief from disgorgement. The Court stated that the first petition for disgorgement is off calendar as moot and since that petition is moot the only matter remaining on calendar is the petition for further disgorgement. The Court stated that the accounting by Mr. Levy needs to be amended, that it needs to include the Swiss bank account, that it is to be filed, served, and noticed for hearing. Mr. Spiegel addressed the non-compliance by Micheal Levy regarding the Court's previous order directing Michael Levy to turn over all of Claude's assets. The Court continued the petition for further disgorgement to November 29, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Michael Levy is to notice his accounting petition for November 29, 2010. Michael Levy inquired about paying Claude's rent. The Court ordered Michael Levy to turn over the money and then Claude can pay his own rent. Mr. Spiegel asked for a "date certain" to turn over the money. The Court stated that the money is to be turned over now.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda.

Petition For Further Relief Regarding Disgorgment For Attorneys Fees And For Damages

Off Calendar Sep-27-2010 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For An Accounting And For Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar Sep-13-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Spiegel for Claude Levy. Michael Levy in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that it was the Court's position that the Power of Attorney has been rescinded, that the Petition to Remove Attorney in Fact is moot and ordered Michael Levy to return all assets belonging to Claude Levy to Claude's Attorney. Michael Levy is to file and serve the accounting by Friday September 17, 2010 with a hearing date of October 4, 2010. Claude Levy's objections to the accounting are due by September 28, 2010. Michael Levy's objections to the Disgorgement Petition/Request for Attorney's Fees are also due by September 28, 2010. Status of filing of the accounting was set for October 4, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

Petition For Disgorgement For Removal Of Attorney In Fact And For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Sep-13-2010 Continued To Oct-04-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For An Accounting And For Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar Aug-09-2010 Continued To Sep-13-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Spiegel for Claude Levy. Michael Levy in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that it was not taking any action in connection with the Israeli estate. However, with regard to the Power of Attorney with Claude and Michael, the Court is prepared to act on the basis of said power of attorney. Mr. Spiegel stated that he was not certain that the information provided is a true and complete accounting of the account. Michael Levy stated that there were no funds in Israel or California, that the funds in New York were equally divided and requested 30 days to hire someone to prepare the accounting in probate form. The matter was continued to September 13, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of filing of the accounting.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Various Bailiffs.
